text: **Anna:** Got it.
**Anna:** Pete, I {promise|約束する|/ˈprɒmɪs/|I promise to help you.} I won't tell anyone.
**Anna:** Bye.
**Anna:** He is so {strange|変な/奇妙な|/streɪndʒ/|That's a strange idea.}
**Ashley:** Hey Anna.
**Anna:** Hi Ashley.
**Anna:** Are you {talking|話している|/ˈtɔːkɪŋ/|We are talking about music.} to yourself again?
**Anna:** No, not this time.
**Anna:** I was {talking|話していた} to Pete.
**Ashley:** How's he doing?
**Anna:** Good.
**Anna:** He has a {birthday|誕生日} coming up, but he told me not to tell anyone.
**Ashley:** Why?
**Anna:** Well, from the way he was speaking
**Anna:** I don't think he likes {cake|ケーキ} or {presents|プレゼント} or fun.
**Ashley:** That sounds like Pete.
**Anna:** Well, I don't care.
**Anna:** I'm getting him a {present|プレゼント}.
**Anna:** Do you know where I can buy something {unique|ユニークな/特別な|/juːˈniːk/|This is a unique gift.}?
**Anna:** I do.
**Ashley:** Tanglewood works
**Anna:** You will definitely find something {unique|ユニークな} there.
**Anna:** Great.
**Anna:** I'll go this weekend.
**Anna:** Now, {speaking|話している} of Pete's birthday, what else should I do?
**Anna:** I know, I'll {rent|借りる} him a {clown|道化師|/klaʊn/|The clown made children laugh.}!
**Ashley:** Yeah.
**Anna:** He'll never {speak|話す} to you again.
**Anna:** No.
**Professor Bot:** Did you hear Ashley and Anna using the words talk and speak?
**Anna:** {Talk|話す/会話する|/tɔːk/|Let's talk about our day.} and {speak|言う/話す|/spiːk/|Can you speak louder?} both mean to say words.
**Anna:** And many times you can use either word without losing any {meaning|意味}.
**Anna:** But there are some {differences|違い} in when we use these words.
**Anna:** The word {talk|話す|/tɔːk/|We talk every morning.} is usually used for {conversations|会話} between two or more people and {informal|くだけた} situations such as between friends or family.
**Anna:** For example, Ashley asks Anna, Are you {talking|話している} to yourself again?
**Anna:** {Speak|話す|/spiːk/|Please speak clearly.} is usually used for one-way communication, such as {presentations|発表} or formal situations such as a boss speaking with her workers.
**Anna:** to talk about language ability and in polite requests.
**Anna:** Keep watching and listen for the words talk and speak
**Sue:** Hey there, {welcome|ようこそ|/ˈwelkəm/|Welcome to our store.} to Tanglewood Works.
**Sue:** I'm Sue.
**Sue:** How can I help you today?
**Anna:** Hi Sue.
**Ashley:** I'm Anna.
**Anna:** A friend told me about your {store|店}.
**Anna:** She said, Anna
**Anna:** This place is really {unique|独特の|/juːˈniːk/|This design is very unique.}
**Sue:** We are.
**Anna:** Here at Tangowood Works, we focus on things that are {handmade|手作りの}, {reclaimed|再利用された}, and {recycled|リサイクルされた}.
**Anna:** Wow, that is really good for the {environment|環境}.
**Sue:** It's really good for you too.
**Anna:** Local {artists|アーティスト} made all of these {one-of-a-kind|唯一無二の} pieces and I paint most of the {furniture|家具}.
**Anna:** Can I look around?
**Sue:** Oh, please do.
**Sue:** So Anna, don't you like to make things?
**Anna:** Me?
**Anna:** Oh no.
**Anna:** Every time I try to make something, something goes wrong.
**Anna:** Oh, oh, sorry.
**Anna:** Sorry.
**Sue:** It's okay.
**Sue:** Anna, everybody can {make|作る/創る|/meɪk/|I make a cake every Sunday.} something.
**Anna:** So this piece is very {interesting|興味深い|/ˈɪntrəstɪŋ/|This book is very interesting.}
**Sue:** You know, when I found these pieces, they were {broken|壊れた/破損した|/ˈbroʊkən/|The vase was broken.} and in a {dumpster|ゴミ捨て場|/ˈdʌmpstər/|We found old furniture in the dumpster.}
**Anna:** But they spoke to me and they said, save me, Sue, save me.
**Anna:** So what do you mean they spoke to you?
**Sue:** When I see something {special|特別な|/ˈspeʃəl/|This is a special moment.} that someone has thrown away, I can almost hear it talk.
**Anna:** Yeah, it's not saying anything.
**Sue:** Anna, it's not easy to see the {treasure|宝/価値あるもの|/ˈtreʒər/|This old coin is a real treasure.} in trash.
**Anna:** Or hear it talk.
**Sue:** But you can learn.
**Anna:** In fact, I teach private classes and one is called Turning Trash to Treasure.
**Sue:** Next week, bring in some trash and we'll turn it into treasure.
**Sue:** Just remember
**Sue:** Pick some trash that speaks to you.
**Anna:** Got it.
**Anna:** I'll see you next week.
**Professor Bot:** Will Anna find trash that speaks to her?
**Anna:** What will it say?
**Anna:** We'll find out next week.
